What's happening everybody? I just wanted to bring a brief little story here and I kind of wanted to shatter some beliefs that I think are espoused a lot in the industry. I mean even not just fitness, kind of a lot of business opportunity, whatever you want to say, you know, make money online, like all that kind of jazz. And it's they're like, don't you just want to run like Donald's, like it's like a machine. But the reality is that like, if you look at the top franchises of any franchise, you have someone behind it who's hustling their butts off, right? And I'm not about the, you know, cool grind life, hustle life. I actually think that that'll make you less money in the long run, sometimes even in the short run. But I mean, there's times for intense work, but a lot of times you need to be kind of making sure that you're executing the right plan. But anyways, so your business is not like a machine, okay? So don't get sold a lie. I think it's much more closely referenced to a garden. And the reason for that is because businesses are far more organic than they are mechanistic, right? They're not, they're not mechanic things, because all they have is they rely on, I mean, biological people, organic people, human beings are the things that run your business just like plants, right? And you don't need to be at your garden all day every day in order for it to look nice. But you do need to work your garden, all right? And so I made some analogies that I thought it's kind of this analogy, one even deeper than I thought it was going to. But like you need to be there to pull out bad weeds, right? Which is bad employees or bad clients, bad attitudes, whatever you want to say, or people who are not aligned with the vision, both clients and employees. And then flowers, you have to water your flowers, right? You have to encourage and recognize your good employees and encourage and recognize your good clients, right? And that's, you know, if someone's a big, a massive refer, like you need to send them gifts, you need to let them know that that kind of behavior is rewarded and not just next week, but today, as in like, if they refer someone to this morning, like you should call them that afternoon to thank them. Because by the third day, like we all assume beings feel like we need recognition for everything that we do. And a lot of times we're always dissatisfied because we're not getting as much recognition as we'd like. And so don't make your business that way, make your business recognition heavy, and give people as much recognition and love as they need, right? Especially if you want to reward a certain type of behavior. Seeding the lawn, you're always going to have to do, you're always going to have to, you know, see the lawns, put new plant new bulbs, and that's going to be the marketing that's going to get more flowers, more good employees, more clients, right? You have to see the lawn. You have to add fertilizer, which is like, how are we going to make the flowers that we have stronger, right? And that's warm traffic plays an ascension. You have to have those things in place so that people stay engaged, so that they grow into even more, you know, flowery plants, right? And the thing is, is that like, I'm not saying that you have to spend all day long weeding your garden, you can. And if you do, then your garden is going to be a lot healthier. But like you can get to the point where you can check your garden a few times a week, make sure that it gets, you know, that it gets fertilized, you can have a team fertilizer, you have to make sure that all these things are being done. And since it is far more organic than it is mechanistic, like a machine, I think that it is much closer to regard than it is a machine. And so just don't get sold a lie. And so don't beat yourself up on the fact like, ah, they're like, make the dream like I never want to have to show up to work every day, because it's not true. Like I still show up to work every day, even though I don't necessarily do all the sales or do all the fulfillment, I still have to run the business. Okay. And so I just want to make this small point, because I think a lot of us are sold this lie. And as soon as I understood it as a garden, it dramatically shifted how I saw my business, my employees and my customers. And it made a huge difference for me. And also just my satisfaction, because I didn't think I was failing all the time, because it wasn't a machine, it didn't run on its own yet. And it never will. All right, so just don't believe that it's really easy to sell, it's very hard to fulfill them. All right, but running it like a garden, having to check a few times a week, that is totally doable. And that is ultimately how you scale this puppy. All right, hope that was useful for you. Hope you got some nuggets there. And as usual, if you want to apply, you're welcome to message and, you know, comment on here or message us anywhere. And we'll obviously hop on the phone with you to chat. So have an awesome day. And I'll catch you guys on the flip side.
